1:
Don't hold onto the handrails! Look forward.
 Step from toe to heel.
 Intervals (30-second sprint + 60-second walk) are more effective than running at a steady pace.
2:
Lie on an incline bench.
 Lower the dumbbells to chest level, keeping your wrists neutrally rotated.
 Pause at the bottom. Press the dumbbells slightly toward each other at the top in an arc.
3:
Lie down with your shoulder blades pulled back and your lower back slightly arched (bridge).
 Lower the bar toward your nipples, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle to your body. Press powerfully.
4:
Standing, lean your body slightly forward (to protect your lower back).
 Keep your elbows slightly bent.
 Raise the dumbbells out to your sides until they are parallel to the floor (no higher, as you risk a shoulder injury).
5:
As you inhale, lower yourself until your elbows are at a 90° angle (or slightly deeper).
 As you exhale, press yourself up.
 Don't let your elbows snap at the top.
 Don't slouch your shoulders.
6:
Starting position: Grasp the pull-up bar with an overhand grip,
 placing your hands slightly wider than your shoulders.
 Straighten your arms fully, hanging from the bar.
 Raise: As you exhale, pull yourself up, aiming to touch the bar with your upper chest.
 Hold: Hold at the top for one second, maximally tensing your back muscles.
7:
Hang, twisting your pelvis upward slightly.
 As you exhale, lift your straight legs until they are parallel to the floor or higher.
 Lower slowly (2-3 seconds).
8:
Keep your elbows firmly at your sides.
 Only your forearms are engaged. Keep your wrists straight.
 At the top, perform a peak contraction for one second.
9:
Standing bent over (back straight), keeping your working shoulder close to your body.
 As you exhale, straighten your arm holding the dumbbell back fully.
 Pause at the top and squeeze your triceps.
10:
Lying on a bench, arms extended upward.
 As you inhale, bend your elbows and lower the bar toward your forehead or behind your head (keep your elbows straight).
 As you exhale, straighten only your elbows.
11:
Standing with the barbell on your shoulders, bend your legs,
 lowering your pelvis until it's parallel to the floor, then return to the top.
12:
Step forward, bend both legs to 90°,
 then push up and return to the starting position.
13:
Standing slightly bent over, holding a barbell or dumbbells,
 push your pelvis back, lowering the weight along your legs to the mid-shin.
14:
Seated on a platform machine, press the platform with your legs, 
 bending and straightening your legs under control.
15: Seated on the machine, extend your legs forward, tensing your quadriceps, then slowly lower them.
16:
Lying or sitting face down, bend your legs,
 pulling the pad toward your glutes, using your hamstrings.
17: Standing on a raised platform, lower your heels down, then rise up onto your toes as high as possible.
18: Placing one foot back on a bench, squat down on your front leg to a 90° angle, then stand back up.
19:
Anchor the loops at the top (above your head).
 Grasp the handles and extend your arms straight out in front of you.
Step forward to create tension. As you inhale, keeping your body in a straight line from head to heels,
 bend your elbows and lower your chest toward your hands, flaring your elbows out to a 45-degree angle.
 As you exhale,powerfully return to the starting position.
20:
Adjust the loops to a medium length (lowered to chest level).
 Grasp the handles and, with your arms straight, lean back with your legs straight—your body should be at an angle, your heels touching the floor.
 As you exhale, bend your elbows and draw your chest toward your hands, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
21:
Stand facing the attachment point of the loops.
 Grasp the handles and extend your arms straight out in front of you (with a slight bend at the elbows).
 Lean back to create tension. On an inhale, keeping your back straight,
 bend your knees and lower yourself into a squat to a 90-degree angle, pushing your pelvis back slightly (as if sitting in a chair).
22: Hang from a rope, pull yourself up using only your arms and core, legs relaxed.
23: Standing between the upper pulleys, bring your straight or slightly bent arms together in front of your chest using the crossed cables.
24: Lying with your lower back on a fitball, twist your torso upward while tensing your abs.
25:
Imitate climbing stairs by alternately pressing the pedals of a stepper.
 With both arms, swing the kettlebell between your legs to chest level,
 using your glutes and back.
26:
With both arms, swing the kettlebell between your legs to chest level,
 using your glutes and back.
27: Rhythmic two-legged jumps over a rotating jump rope.
28: Pull the expander handles to your temples while rotating your wrists and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
29: Lying face down on a Roman chair, straighten your torso upward while bracing your back and glutes.
